title: Fixing Broken HTML Anchor Tags with Missing 'href' Attributes

statement.markdown: Given a string representing an HTML anchor tag, validate if the 'href' attribute exists and is set to a valid URL. If valid, return the anchor tag with the 'href' attribute; otherwise, return an empty string. The function should handle multiple occurrences of anchor tags in the input string.
statement.inputFormat: A string containing one or more HTML anchor tags separated by spaces.
statement.outputFormat: A string containing the anchor tag(s) with a valid 'href' attribute, or an empty string if no anchor tags have a valid 'href' attribute.
statement.notes: A valid URL is one that starts with 'http://' or 'https://'.

constraints.description: The number of anchor tags in the input string.

examples.explanation: The second anchor tag is missing the 'href' attribute.
The first anchor tag is missing the 'href' attribute.
Both anchor tags have a valid 'href' attribute.

hints.text: Use a regular expression to extract the 'href' attribute from each anchor tag.
